reasoning_content: We need to check if 389 is a prime number. A quick sanity check: check divisibility by small primes: 2 (no, odd), 3 (sum digits 3+8+9=20, not divisible by 3), 5 (not ending in 0/5), 7? 7*55=385, remainder 4, not divisible. 11? 11*35=385, remainder 4; 11*36=396 too high. 13? 13*29=377, remainder 12; 13*30=390 too high. 17? 17*22=374, remainder 15; 17*23=391 too high. 19? 19*20=380, remainder 9; 19*21=399 >389. 23? 23*16=368, remainder 21; 23*17=391 too high. So we need to check up to sqrt(389) ≈ 19.7, so primes up to 19 are enough. We've checked 2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19. None divide. So 389 is prime. Answer: prime.
